entry.title: ALMathon Americas 2026: Innovation Across the Americas
entry.content:
The ALMathon continues its journey into its sixth year — and for the first time with a dedicated Americas edition! This regional hackathon for SAP Partners is a key element of SAP Cloud ALM's Partner strategy and closely tied to the flagship event ALM Summit NA 2026 in Newtown Square.

The 2026 Americas Challenge

The mission: Create innovative use cases to extend SAP Cloud ALM — with a strong focus on embedding AI into ALM processes to support smarter implementations, optimized operations, and outcome-driven transformation.

 

Since the kick-off on January 14th, 9 dedicated partner teams have taken on this challenge. During the Ideate phase, teams shaped their ideas before moving into the Build phase starting February 1st. With questions and support on demand from SAP Cloud ALM Product Experts, they have been brainstorming, coding, and integrating their solutions.

On March 20th, all teams presented their solutions in the Pitch Semi-Finals —  covering Problem, Solution, Demo, and Technology. An expert jury of 6 SAP judges evaluated each pitch across three categories: Innovation, Value, and Creativity.

The Top 3 teams have now been selected and will present their solutions live on stage at the ALM Summit NA on April 15th, 2026, in Newtown Square. Congratulations to the finalists:

  • BCS – Business Core Solutions
  • The Purrfect Connectors – Vostura GmbH
  • TestAgents – SmartBear

While we celebrate the finalists, we also want to recognize the dedication of all participating teams. All 9 innovative solutions developed during the ALMathon Americas 2026 are showcased here:

BCS – Business Core Solutions

Solution: Public Cloud Configuration Automation Using SAP Cloud ALM

Solution Description: BCS's solution uses SAP Cloud ALM Project Tasks to trigger automated public cloud configuration via Symphony Orchestration Platform. Configuration results are automatically documented back in SAP Cloud ALM for full transparency and traceability. This creates a closed-loop process from planning to execution, eliminating manual handovers between tasks and teams.

Problem Addressed: Public-cloud configuration is still manual-heavy and error-prone when executed across multiple tasks and teams. There is a lack of end-to-end traceability between what was planned in project tasks and what was actually configured in the system.

Innovative Aspect: The solution combines ALM-driven automation with closed-loop transparency and GenAI-assisted orchestration, bridging the gap between project planning and technical execution.

Technologies Used: SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Public Cloud, and Symphony Orchestration Platform.

 

HR Path Brasil

Solution: Integration Monitoring Enhancing for HCM

Solution Description: HR Path Brasil's solution extends the existing integration monitoring capabilities in SAP Cloud ALM by adding special features tailored to help users working with HCM solutions. The application enhances error analysis with greater detail, making it easier for teams to identify and resolve integration issues specific to HCM landscapes.

Problem Addressed: There is currently a lack of details when analyzing errors in integration monitoring for HCM, making it difficult for teams to quickly diagnose and fix issues in their HCM integration flows.

Innovative Aspect: The solution leverages AI to improve the user experience in error analysis and monitoring, bringing intelligence to a traditionally manual troubleshooting process.

Technologies Used: SuccessFactors and SAP BTP.

 

MoveOn CoE SAP – Move.On Business Transformation

Solution: Clean Core Studio

Solution Description: Clean Core Studio is an extension that continuously monitors S/4HANA development objects and configurations, comparing them against functional specifications defined in SAP Cloud ALM. Using AI Foundation, it automatically evaluates if implementations follow Clean Core best practices, creates defects when violations are detected, and provides a dashboard showing compliance evolution over time.

Problem Addressed: SAP implementation projects have functional specifications (Requirements) in Cloud ALM, but there is no automated validation that the technical implementation follows Clean Core guidelines. This gap leaves compliance verification as a manual, error-prone activity.

Innovative Aspect: The solution bridges the gap between functional specifications and technical implementation by using AI to extract compliance criteria from natural language requirements and automatically evaluate ABAP objects against Clean Core rules.

Technologies Used: SAP Cloud ALM APIs (Tasks, Analytics), SAP BTP (Cloud Foundry), SAP CAP (Node.js), SAP AI Foundation, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Fiori Elements, SAP Cloud Connector, S/4HANA RFC/ADT APIs.

 

 

 

Novatégica

Solution: Sales Simulator

Solution Description: Novatégica's Sales Simulator is a new application built on SAP BTP that exposes product prices to customers through a web front-end directly integrated to SAP ERP. It enables customers to view prices of pre-orders without requiring calls to the sales team, streamlining the sales process.

Problem Addressed: Users need to know the prices of pre-orders but currently rely on manual interaction with sales teams to obtain this information, creating unnecessary delays and overhead.

Innovative Aspect: The solution provides a customer-facing web front-end directly integrated to SAP ERP, allowing self-service price visibility and reducing dependency on the sales team.

Technologies Used: SAP BTP, SAP SD.

 

ReleaseOwl, Inc

Note: ReleaseOwl, Inc participated in the ALMathon Americas 2026 with two separate solutions, both showcased below.

Solution: Feature Release Readiness Check: Security • Architecture • Quality — with ReleaseOwl AI Coach

Solution Description: ReleaseOwl's AI Coach evaluates a feature change, flags security, architecture, and quality risks, and produces a release-ready action list before the change moves forward. SAP Cloud ALM stays the system of record, while ReleaseOwl operationalizes execution with standardized pipelines, controls, and proof.

Problem Addressed: SAP Cloud ALM provides governance truth — planning, traceability, testing visibility — but many programs struggle to realize full value because release execution and evidence remain manual and inconsistent.

Innovative Aspect: The AI Coach performs a "Feature Release Readiness Check," automatically assessing security, architecture, and quality aspects of a change. Cloud ALM remains the system of record while ReleaseOwl adds operationalized execution with standardized pipelines, approvals, and evidence packs.

Technologies Used: SAP Cloud ALM APIs / Integration (release/change/task context, traceability links), ReleaseOwl platform (pipeline execution, approvals, evidence packs, reporting), AI Coach for ABAP (AI-assisted release readiness assessment: security, architecture, quality).


2. Solution: AI-Powered Quality Gates for Integration Suite

Solution Description: This solution demonstrates how SAP Cloud ALM release intent is operationalized for SAP Integration Suite changes using ReleaseOwl quality gates. It features automated change-risk scoring, smart regression test generation/execution from traces, and an AI-Ops anomaly signal summary — ending with an evidence output linked back to Cloud ALM for release governance and audit readiness.

Problem Addressed: Regression coverage is hard to maintain as iFlows change, failures are detected late (post-deploy) requiring firefighting, and evidence for what was tested, what changed, and why it was approved is scattered across different systems.

Innovative Aspect: AI-powered quality gates for Integration Suite that run every time a change is promoted, ensuring automated validation and evidence collection throughout the release process.

Technologies Used: SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Integration Suite / Cloud Integration (CPI), ReleaseOwl, AI modules.

 

Team Mygo – Mygo Consulting inc.

Solution: nxYoda – SAP Solutions made simpler

Solution Description: nxYoda addresses one of the biggest challenges faced by IT teams — understanding the existing solution alongside building new ones. It provides a valuable set of agents that can help build new solutions and shorten SDLC. nxYoda uses Document repositories, test cases, and feature documentation from SAP Cloud ALM as the source of data and is also capable of pushing test scripts and specifications back to Cloud ALM. This adds much needed Gen AI features on top of the robust repository functionality provided by SAP Cloud ALM.

Problem Addressed: Understanding existing solutions that may have been a result of multiple projects, understanding the impact of new changes being introduced, and longer SDLC cycles — leading to longer time taken to design, document, develop, and test the applications.

Innovative Aspect: Leverages generative AI to help build new solutions and help organizations scale at a rapid pace, using an AI agent advisor to accelerate the software development lifecycle.

Technologies Used: Next.js, Node.js, OpenAI LLM, SAP BTP, PostgreSQL.

 

 

TestAgents – SmartBear

Solution: Agentic test case generation

Solution Description: TestAgents' solution reads SAP process documents or any requirements document describing a critical SAP process that needs to be tested, and automatically creates fully automated tests through an agentic, chat-like interface with minimal input from the user. The tests are synced to SAP ALM and execution results are displayed there. Multiple agents can run in parallel if needed.

Problem Addressed: Test automation is still a major bottleneck as companies transition to the cloud. Users need to create tests manually, and existing solutions are cumbersome and clunky. The solution aims to eliminate the complexity of automated test creation and execution.

Innovative Aspect: This is true agentic behavior — users only need to upload a process flow document and provide additional context about what needs to be tested and the overall intent. The system autonomously generates and executes the tests.

Technologies Used: Claude, Gemini 3.0, MCP, SAP Cloud ALM APIs.

 

The Purrfect Connectors – Vostura GmbH

Solution: cocat powered by Vostura - no-code integration platform for SAP Cloud ALM

Solution Description: cocat is a SAP BTP-based integration hub that enables an integrated toolchain between SAP Cloud ALM and multiple third-party tools such as ServiceNow, Jira, and others. It provides standardized, reusable integration patterns and governance capabilities through a single Cloud ALM endpoint.

Problem Addressed: SAP Cloud ALM currently supports only a single integration endpoint per project and artifact type, which prevents parallel integrations with multiple third-party tools. Additionally, integrating Cloud ALM with external tools often requires custom implementations.

Innovative Aspect: Enabling true multi-tool ALM toolchains through a single Cloud ALM endpoint, combined with standardized, reusable integration patterns and governance capabilities.

Technologies Used: SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), SAP Cloud ALM APIs & Events.

 


We are looking forward to seeing all three finalists pitch their solutions live on stage at the ALM Summit NA on April 15th, 2026, in Newtown Square. The winner will be decided by the votes of all summit participants. We want to thank all 7 participating teams for their dedication, creativity, and innovation — you are shaping the future of SAP Cloud ALM together with us.

We hope that all solutions will be soon available to every customer and partner on SAP Store.

 

Interested in joining the next ALMathon? Just drop an email to alm_partner@sap.com.

entry.title: 1H 2026 - Release highlights of SuccessFactors Succession
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The 1H 2026 release is here!

A new set of enhancements has been released in the 1H 2026 release that has been eagerly awaited. The SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release includes some key features and enhancements, so let's take a closer look!

1. Filter Positions with Generic Objects and Foundation Objects in Position Tile Live Slides:

Generic Object and Foundation Object filters are now supported in Position Tile live slides within Presentations.

When creating or editing Position Tile live slides in Presentations, users can now view and select values for Generic Object and Foundation Object filters. Previously, though they appeared as filter options, they did not display values for selection. The filter options displayed in Presentations mirror the options you configured for Position Tile View.

This enhancement filled the functional gap between Presentations and the Position Tile View, allowing users to efficiently filter and present positions in Presentations.

      Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've added Generic Object and Foundation Object fields as searchable fields to the Position object.
  • You've enabled the setting Include Generic Objects and Foundation Objects in filters in the Position Tile view in Admin Center>Succession Settings.

2. Permission-Based Visibility of Performance Ratings in AI-Assisted Successor Insights:

As an administrator, you can now use role-based permissions to control the visibility of overall performance ratings in AI-assisted successor insights, preventing sensitive information from being exposed to unauthorized users.

On a position card in the Succession Org Chart or the Position Tile view, succession planners can leverage generative AI capabilities to generate insights into successors nominated for the position or the incumbent. Previously, the insights always display the nominees' latest overall performance ratings. Now, only succession planners with the User Permissions>Employee Data>Background Information permission for the element <sysOverallPerformance> can view those data of their target population. Ratings of users out of the target populations, as shown in the screenshot, are not available for AI analysis.

Enablement: Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've purchased the AI unit’s license. For more information about AI units license, please contact your Account Executive.
  • You've selected the option Assisted Successor Insights in Admin Center>AI Services Administration.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Users have the User Permissions>AI Access>Assisted Successor Insights permission to access the AI-assisted Successor Insights feature.
  • Users have the User Permissions>Employee Data>Background Information permission for the element <sysOverallPerformance> to access the target population's overall performance rating data.

3. Use Custom Attributes in Talent Search:

Users can now add custom attributes as search options in Talent Search, in addition to skills and competencies.

In the talent search results page, they can then view the name and proficiency level of custom attributes that an employee has and compare employees side by side. They can also select custom attributes to be displayed in the exported search results file.

When reloading saved search criteria, inactive attributes are automatically removed from the pre-filled filters to prevent errors or confusion.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • Your organization has migrated to Talent Intelligence Hub.
  • The status of the attributes and their attribute types must be active.
  • In Talent Search Settings, the option Show Attributes is selected, and a rating scale is selected from the dropdown list for All Attributes.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Users have the User Permissions>Growth Portfolio>Attribute>View permission.
  • Users have the User Permissions>Growth Portfolio>Attribute Type>View permission.
  • Users have the User Permissions>Growth Portfolio>Proficiency Level Scale>View permission.
  • Users have the User Permissions>Succession Planners>Talent Search Access permission.
  • Users have the User Permissions>Talent Search Field>Ratings>Attribute permission.

For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

entry.title: Запис вебінару "Відповідність рішень SAP вимогам українського законодавства та обліку"
entry.content:
Дякуємо всім, хто доєднався до вебінару «SAP для України: відповідність рішень SAP вимогам українського законодавства та обліку» в рамках березневих онлайн‑подій SAP Україна. Ця зустріч стала частиною серії заходів, яку ми присвятили хмарі, даним, AI та підтримці користувачів.

Що обговорили на вебінарі

Під час сесії ми показали, як отримати максимум від SAP Support та SAP for Me, зокрема:

  • інфраструктуру SAP Support: які інструменти й сервіси доступні клієнтам;

  • управління користувачами та роботу з обліковими записами в SAP for Me;

  • завантаження ПЗ, роботу з ліцензіями та ключовими параметрами системної адміністрації;

  • сповіщення, сервісні запити та роль Customer Interaction Center (CIC) у щоденній взаємодії із SAP.

Це практична сесія для тих, хто відповідає за підтримку користувачів, стабільність ландшафту SAP та ефективну роботу з інструментами підтримки.

Запис вебінару доступний

Якщо ви не встигли приєднатися наживо або хочете повернутися до окремих блоків, запис вебінару вже доступний онлайн.

Дивіться у зручний час, діліться лінком з колегами та використовуйте отримані інсайти, щоб зробити вашу взаємодію з SAP Support ще прозорішою та ефективнішою.

entry.title: 1H 2026 - Release highlights of SuccessFactors Calibration
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The 1H 2026 release is here!

A new set of enhancements has been released in the 1H 2026 release that has been eagerly awaited. The SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release includes some key features and enhancements, so let's take a closer look!

1. Automatic Bin Width Adjustment in Bin View:

When users hide bins in bin view, the remaining bins are now automatically resized to equal widths for a consistent layout.

We made this enhancement to reduce the need for manual resizing and maintain a consistent and organized view when users customize the bin display.

      Enablement: Automatically on

2. Disable Calibration Home Page Card by Role:

The new setting Disable Calibration Home Page Card Generation by Role is available under Admin Center>Manage Calibration Settings. This allows you to select specific roles, such as facilitator, co-facilitator, owner, participant, and executive reviewer, that are not supposed to receive the “Complete Calibration” card on their home page. When enabled, users in these roles will not see calibration cards on their home page but will maintain access and edit permissions for calibration sessions. This setting only affects sessions activated after it has been enabled. Existing cards for sessions that were activated prior to enabling this setting will continue to be displayed.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

You've enabled Disable Calibration Home Page Card Generation by Role under Admin Center>Manage Calibration Settings>Global Settings and selected specific roles.

3. Enable Matrix Managers to Create Sessions on Calibration Page:

Matrix managers can now create sessions directly on the Calibration page using the Create New button. During session creation, they can select their matrix reports as session participants and choose their matrix reports or lower-level employees as session subjects.

This enhancement is from customer idea 301002Information published on SAP site. It allows matrix managers to independently manage calibration sessions, improving process efficiency.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

You've enabled Enable Session Creation for Direct and Matrix Managers under the Global Settings tab at Admin Center>Manage Calibration Settings. Previously, this option was called Enable Manager Calibration Session.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You've granted managers the User Permissions>Calibration>Enable Session Creation for Direct and Matrix Managers permission and selected which templates they can use for session creation. Previously, the permission was called Manager Template Permission for Creating Sessions.

4. Enable Matrix Managers to Create Sessions on Calibration Page:

We've made enhancements to the filter settings in calibration sessions.

Here are the details:

  • In a calibration session, your filter settings for subject selection are now saved automatically. When you leave and later return to the same calibration session, your previously applied filters are automatically restored, and the subject list remains filtered as before.
  • When you apply filters to the subject list, the number of subjects that match your filter criteria is now displayed next to the filter icon. This allows you to quickly check how many subjects have been filtered out based on your selections.

We made these enhancements to reduce the need for repetitive setup when returning to the same calibration session and provide immediate visibility into the filtering results.

Enablement: Automatically on

5. Enable Matrix Managers to Create Sessions on Calibration Page:

You can now add up to 3000 subjects, including inactive users, to a single session, allowing reviewers to calibrate ratings for larger groups in one session. Previously, the limit was 2000 subjects. Besides, the maximum number of subjects that can be displayed without filtering in a single view has increased from 2000 to 3000.

This enhancement is from customer idea 216483 . It improves user management efficiency for large organizations with extensive user bases.

Enablement: Automatically on

6. Disable Calibration Home Page Card by Role:

You can now edit the section details of a talent card in the latest People Profile dialog. Editing is not available for the Career Goals and Variable Pay History Data sections as they are not supported in the latest People Profile.

Enablement: Customer configured 

Configuration Requirements:

You've configured the corresponding custom background element information in the latest People Profile.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You have View and Edit permissions for the respective Employee Data sections under User Permissions>Employee Data.

7. Mark Subjects as Discussed Directly in List View:

You can now directly mark subjects as discussed in the List view using a new dropdown menu.

This enhancement is from customer idea 214414, Information published on the SAP site, and customer idea 346278, Information published on the SAP site. It saves time for reviewers during calibration by simplifying the process of marking subjects as discussed.

Enablement: Automatically on

For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

entry.title: 'Transaction canceled (SO 868)' in SM21 logs of SAP system
entry.content:
Hi, 

You see error 'Transaction canceled (SO 868)' in SM21 logs in SAP system.

 

And SM21 Documentation says:

 

Message Text: Transaction canceled SO 868 ( )

 

The transaction has been terminated.  This may be caused by a

termination message from the application (MESSAGE Axxx) or by an

error detected by the SAP System due to which it makes no sense to

proceed with the transaction.  The actual reason for the termination

is indicated by the T100 message and the parameters.

Solution:

This error message usually comes because there is authorization problem.

 

 

To verify whether a missing authorization is causing the issue during the Datasphere extraction, please perform an authorization trace for the technical user <UserName> using transaction STAUTH_TRACE (or STAUTHTRACE, depending on your system).
Run the trace while executing the task chain.

entry.title: Re: he supplier down payment app is currently not visible...
entry.content:
Hi @paresh31837, 

as an addition to @Chris1973’s helpful input:


The App Manage Supplier Down Payment Request (F1688) is part of Business Catalog Accounts Payable and Receivable - Down Payments (SAP_FIN_BC_APAR_DP_REQ_PC).

 

A prerequisite for the activation of this app is the activation of one of the Scope Items:

  • For USA: Process Navigator O59 - SAP for Me; Process Navigator O60 - SAP for Me
  • For all other countries: Process Navigator J59 - SAP for Me; Process Navigator J60 - SAP for Me

 

After scope activation, the business catalog can be added to the relevant role(s) via app Maintain Business Roles.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite automatic deployments using Ansible
entry.content:
Introduction 

Ansible was included in the architecture with the sole purpose of automating repetitive deployment tasks, reducing the effort, the time spent, restricting the roles/accesses in the the productive tenant, and most important, human errors. By creating only once a file with instructions, Ansible will be able to go through each SAP environment, dynamically execute the steps mentioned, configure parameters, create credentials, deploy artifacts, etc. 

When transporting artifact changes from DEV up to PROD, we, as developers, might need to configure manually, for each system, some of the following: 

  • Users Credential Creation  
  • Updating Known Hosts 
  • Service Keys in SAP Business Technology Platform 
  • Updating external parameters  
  • Import certificates 
  • Deploying integration artifacts  
  • Adding JDBC Material 
  • Configuring Trading Partner Management / API Management 
  • Other 

By using this automated approach, the developers will set up a list of instructions only once, and they will be able to execute these steps as many times as needed.  

Architecture Overview 

Picture 1 - Architecture Overview

 

ServiceNow and Github as source of truth 

For the sake of observability and audit, we wanted to have as much control as possible and be able to see who did what, when, and how. 

With this in mind, we decided that ServiceNow Change Requests together with Github, will be our sources of truth. 

 

  1. Each ServiceNow CHG will contain the Ansible instructions file. 
  1. Each ServiceNow CHG will contain the Jenkins build log. 
  1. Github will hold all the generated Ansible playbooks, failed and successful instructions. 
  1. Github will hold all the sensitive data ( certificates, credentials, etc. ), encrypted using ansible-vault. 

How it works?  

Ansible will start right after the transport stage is done, so the latest artifact version is present in the target environment. 

It will look for a special instructions file in ServiceNow CHGs, and in case it finds it, it starts the process. This instruction file is YAML format, something that can be easily understood by humans, and at the same time by programs. Ansible goes through all steps mentioned in the file and executes them one by one. 

Picture 2 - Sample Ansible Task

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Each step has a list of parameters and a reference to something called a 'task'.  

For us, tasks are API calls, like we do in Postman – set the url, set the body, headers, authentication, etc. 

For Ansible, tasks are logic blocks which contain full details about the API call, can receive a list of parameters to iterate over, can validate response codes, can extract values from the response body, etc. 

Wait. We don't use the same parameters in all environments. 

Sometimes we do, sometimes we don't. For scenarios where the parameters are the same, you can just import and start the pipeline, and the job is done. 

For scenarios where the parameters are different (the usual cases):  

  • Import the yaml, where the whole list of steps is ALREADY defined 
  • Modify the different parameters (Change '100' to '200' for sap-client f.e. ) 
  • Start the pipeline 
  • Done 

Ansible will take care of the actions done on the SAP Integration Suite tenants, it will create security materials, it will save everything in Github, it will log everything in Jenkins, it will validate what you filled in the steps. No more typos, no more errors, no more forgetting to add a certificate, a ssh key, forgot to deploy, etc. 

I am a developer, how do I use it? 

The first step would be to go into the template generator page and check all the needed configuration steps. 

Picture 3 - Template Page

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

After all steps are configured as per the needs of the ServiceNow Change Request, the developer will just review the generated template and push it into the  

Change Request. The template will be automatically pushed into the ServiceNow Change Request, as an attachment, consolidating traceability. 

Picture 4 - Template preview

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

After that, using Jenkins, he can trigger a pipeline to start Ansible and view the logs.  

Picture 5 - SNOW Attachments

 

 

 

 

 

 

Below is a sample Jenkins build log, in which you will see how Ansible is triggered to get the last modified date of a package and create a service key in our SAP Business Technology Platform subaccount.

[Pipeline] echo 
EXECUTING ANSIBLE PLAYBOOK 
[Pipeline] echo 
Playbook: implementation_steps_CHG047545_preprod.yml 
[Pipeline] echo 
Environment: preprod 
[Pipeline] echo 
Ansible Host: localhost:2345 
[Pipeline] echo 
Triggered By: [Admin] 
[Pipeline] echo 
Start Time: 2026-02-12 14:10:24 
[Pipeline] echo 
============================================================ 
[Pipeline] httpRequest 
HttpMethod: POST 
URL: http://localhost:2345/run-playbook/implementation_steps_CHG047545_preprod.yml 
Content-Type: application/json 
Sending request to url: http://localhost:2345/run-playbook/implementation_steps_CHG047545_preprod.yml 
Response Code: HTTP/1.1 200 OK 
Response:  
[WARNING]: Found variable using reserved name: environment 
 
PLAY [Build #22 -- Playbook for CHG047545_preprod] ***************************** 
 
TASK [Task 0 - Initialize global env variable] ********************************* 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [DEBUG - Check environment value] ***************************************** 
ok: [localhost] => { 
    "msg": "The value of environment is 'preprod' and global_env_variable is 'preprod'" 
} 
 
TASK [Task 1 - Initialize global aggregator] *********************************** 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [Task 2 - Execute Authentication] ***************************************** 
 
TASK [0.1 - Get OAuth2 Access Token] ******************************************* 
 
TASK [tools : Step 1 - POST Call for fetching OAuth2 Access Token.] ************ 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [tools : Step 2 - Extracting access_token from the json response.] ******** 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [0.2 - Get CPI API X-CSRF-Token] ****************************************** 
 
TASK [tools : Step 1 - GET Call for fetching X-CSRF-Token.] ******************** 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [tools : Step 2 - Extracting X-CSRF-Token from json response.] ************ 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [auth_helper : 0.3 - Debug current tokens] ******************************** 
skipping: [localhost] 
 
TASK [Task 3 - Execute get-package-last-modified] ****************************** 
 
TASK [integration-flow : Task 1 - Get Integration Package by ID and return the integration package] *** 
ok: [localhost] => (item=SAPCPIPackage1) 
 
TASK [integration-flow : Task 2 - Extract response from x.msg.results[0].json.d] *** 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [integration-flow : Task 3 - Display the last modified date (dd/mm/yyyy HH:MM:SS)] *** 
ok: [localhost] => { 
    "msg": "Last modified date: 02/02/2026 18:15:12" 
} 
 
TASK [Task 4 - Execute get_service_instance_by_name] *************************** 
 
TASK [Task 1 - Get CF OAuth Token] ********************************************* 
 
TASK [tools : Task 1 - Get CF OAuth Token] ************************************* 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [tools : Task 2 - Print OK if token was retrieved successfully] *********** 
ok: [localhost] => { 
    "msg": "OAuth token retrieval: OK" 
} 
 
TASK [tools : Task 3 - Print NOT OK if token retrieval failed] ***************** 
skipping: [localhost] 
 
TASK [tools : Task 4 - Extract access_token from cf_oauth_token_response] ****** 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [cf-api : Task 2 - Get Service Instance by Name] ************************** 
ok: [localhost] => (item=None) 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [cf-api : Task 3 - Return message ok if status code is 200 or 202] ******** 
ok: [localhost] => (item=SK-ProcessIntegrationRuntime) => { 
    "msg": "Service Instance found successfully for SK-ProcessIntegrationRuntime" 
} 
 
TASK [cf-api : Task 4 - Return message error if status code is not 200 or 202] *** 
skipping: [localhost] => (item=SK-ProcessIntegrationRuntime)  
skipping: [localhost] 
 
TASK [cf-api : Task 5 - Extract resources.guid from service_instance] ********** 
ok: [localhost] => (item=None) 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [Task 5 - Execute create_service_key] ************************************* 
 
TASK [cf-api : Task 0 - Build service key body] ******************************** 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [Task 1 - Get CF OAuth Token] ********************************************* 
 
TASK [tools : Task 1 - Get CF OAuth Token] ************************************* 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [tools : Task 2 - Print OK if token was retrieved successfully] *********** 
ok: [localhost] => { 
    "msg": "OAuth token retrieval: OK" 
} 
 
TASK [tools : Task 3 - Print NOT OK if token retrieval failed] ***************** 
skipping: [localhost] 
 
TASK [tools : Task 4 - Extract access_token from cf_oauth_token_response] ****** 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [cf-api : Task 2 - Create Service Key] ************************************ 
ok: [localhost] 
 
TASK [cf-api : Task 3 - Return message ok if status code is 200 or 202] ******** 
ok: [localhost] => { 
    "msg": "Service Key created successfully" 
} 
 
TASK [cf-api : Task 4 - Return message error if status code is not 200 or 202] *** 
skipping: [localhost] 
 
PLAY RECAP ********************************************************************* 
localhost                  : ok=22   changed=0    unreachable=0    failed=0    skipped=5    rescued=0    ignored=0    
 
 
============================================================ 
EXECUTION SUMMARY: 
Playbook: implementation_steps_CHG047545_preprod.yml 
Environment: preprod 
Duration: 7343ms 
Exit Code: 0 
Status: SUCCESS 
============================================================ 
 
Success code from [200‥200] 
[Pipeline] echo 
 
============================================================ 
[Pipeline] echo 
EXECUTION COMPLETED

Reusability 

Picture 6 - Reusability

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

If the developer wants to continue to the next environment, it can just import the previously generated template on the UI template-generator page, change the environment (replace 'test' with 'preprod' in a dropdown) and push the template again. 

Available actions 

We have converted the whole API spec of SAP Integration Suite into dynamic, modular Ansible tasks. Basically anything that is available on SAP Business Accelerator Hub, can be triggered using Ansible.  

 

Picture 7 - Available Tasks

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Security and encryption 

In the instructions file, visible in the ServiceNow attachments and Github storage, we will only see references, no plain text credentials, certificates, API tokens, etc. 

All these references are being used to extract the actual data from a CHG-specific vars file, along with the global vars credentials shared across all ServiceNow Change Request. 

These vars files are using a standard functionality of Ansible, called Ansible Vault. 

Ansible Vault relies on the following cryptographic components: 

  • Cipher: AES-256 in CTR (Counter) mode. 
  • Key Derivation: It uses PBKDF2 (Password-Based Key Derivation Function 2) to turn our plain-text passwords into a high-entropy encryption key. 
  • Hashing: HMAC-SHA256.  
  • Salt: A random salt is generated for every encryption operation, ensuring that the same password and the same plain-text result in a different cipher text every time. 

For example, if we want to deploy a JDBC material  

Picture 9 - Add JDBC

 

 

 

 

 

We will use the CPISNOW_PREPROD reference in the instruction file. This address, along with its details, will be added automatically in that CHGs vars file  

Picture 10 - Vars file

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Above you can see how the vars file looks decrypted. It is a dummy JDBC server, in which all the required fields to deploy the JDBC material are filled. By using a vault pass file, Ansible will decrypt, extract and encrypt back all the files whenever they are needed. Besides that, everything is secured and maintained correctly. 

Auditing & Traceability 

Traceability

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

By saving these recipes as code, they can be subject to an automatic/manual code review process, they can be traced to whoever triggered the pipelines and they're auditable. At any given moment, we have access to the entire workflow – we can see the configured steps, we can see the parameters that were added, we can see the Git commit logs, we can see timestamps, authors, etc. 

Conclusions 

Using automated deployments, the error rate would significantly decrease, since the developer will not go manually and repeat the steps on each environment. The only place where it would be able to make any sort of mistakes would be when generating the template, but even so, the html form contains a lot of validations, and also the dropdowns are dynamically generated with actual data from the system, so it would minimize the error rate. 

Including Github and Jenkins, everything is logged, everything is tracked, everything can be reverted and, in the end, the developers would not even need edit roles in PREPROD/PROD, because every configuration will be done through the technical user. 

 

 

If you have any questions, feel free to reply here, or even go to the dedicated Q&A Section for SAP Integration Suite.

Thank you for your time !

entry.title: Re: Guide: Step By Step Procedure To Send And Receive Messages in a SAP Event Mesh Instance
entry.content:
Hi @sanj22,

the interesting part of the service descriptor is the rules section:

"rules": {
    "queueRules": {
      "publishFilter": [
        "${namespace}/q1",
        "${namespace}/q2/+/x",
        "other/namespace/id/q1"
      ],
      "subscribeFilter": [
        "${namespace}/q1",
        "${namespace}/q2/*"
      ]
    },
    "topicRules": {
      "publishFilter": [
        "${namespace}/a",
        "${namespace}/a/+/b/*"
      ],
      "subscribeFilter": [
        "${namespace}/a/b/*",
        "+/+/+/a/b/*"
      ]
    }
  }

 see also here.

entry.title: Re: Add metadata in SAP BPA Mail Body
entry.content: Hello RomnickJohn, in SAP Build Process Automation, metadata or process context values cannot be inserted directly inside the URL field of the mail body’s hyperlink dialog because the link editor does not parse or replace placeholders like {{InstanceId}}. This is a known limitation of the BPA Mail Body editor. The only reliable workaround is to first construct the complete URL including the metadata in the main mail body text area by inserting the metadata from the Value Binding panel instead of typing it manually. Once the dynamic URL appears correctly in the text (for example, https://myapp.com/instances/ {{ProcessMetadata.InstanceId}}), highlight the entire combined text and apply the hyperlink formatting, pasting the same combined text into the link field. This ensures the metadata is resolved at runtime even though the link dialog itself cannot process placeholders. Hope this helps you!

entry.title: Re: Data Replication framework- (DRF)
entry.content:
Hi @Srikanth_Jutur ,

I am working on Data Replication Framework (DRF) in SAP S/4HANA and I have a requirement to replicate data from table T880 (Company master data) to an external system.

As per my understanding, T880 is not available as a standard DRF business object.

I would like to know:

  1. How can we create a custom DRF business object for table T880?

  2. What are the steps to create a custom outbound implementation for this table?

  3. How to implement the outbound class and integrate it with DRF?

  4. What is the recommended approach in real projects — should we use DRF or a custom interface for such configuration tables?

Any guidance or sample implementation would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: I want to process approvals using a UI screen customi...
entry.content:
Hello @koide_yuya ,

Yes, it’s possible to use a custom UI5 application within SAP Build Process Automation. The Standard plan fully supports this capability.

Simply deploy your UI5 application to SAP BTP, then take the App ID and Version from its manifest.json and register it in BPA as a Custom UI Form. BPA will embed the deployed UI5 screen exactly as it is — so any styling, layout changes, or UI enhancements must be implemented directly within your UI5 project.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: The Value of Implementing SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity
entry.content:
SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity Capabilities

Ever found yourself wondering what are all the different capabilities of SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity? If so, we will start with a quick overview. The capabilities of SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity are continually increasing, evolving and it might be difficult to stay on top of these dynamic developments.

In terms of widest availability globally, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity has full support of connectivity via Swift. Swift is a global secure messaging network that enables banks and corporates to exchange standardized financial messages, such as payments, security transactions, and treasury instructions. SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity supports the full range of Swift capabilities via Alliance Lite 2 through the service channels FIN, FIN+/Score+ (Interact) or FileAct.

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity differentiates direct bank connections in two ways: member banks and non-member banks. Let’s first focus on banks with a direct relationship. The onboarding process is aligned upfront with the bank, and thus the number of details you need to provide to setup the connections is reduced. Also, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity can connect to APIs provided by member banks to cover additional scenarios like instant balances. You can find the supported capabilities and services on #Member Banks | SAP Help Portal.  

Second option for bank connections is with non-member banks. This is usually done via an SFTP server (H2H) connection, or via their EBICS Server.  An additional connection option is EBICS (Electronic Banking Internet Communication Standard), which was created by the German central bank as a secure protocol for transmitting payment files, account statements, and other financial messages directly between corporates and banks. It is most widely used in the German-speaking countries and parts of the CEE region. For all connection types, the customer can decide which connection with their banks they want and an agreement between the bank and the customer is required as well. Otherwise, the connection via SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity wouldn’t be accepted by the bank. This is typically a step the customer and the bank do before an implementation project on customer side is starting.  

We are also excited to share with you that SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity provides a service channel for the SAP Digital Currency Hub! SAP Digital Currency Hub facilitates cross-border payments by enabling corporates to use blockchain-based stablecoins and central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) in a standardized, integrated way within SAP systems, reducing intermediaries and settlement time.

Picture: Overview of SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity capabilities


Benefits of Implementing SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity

One of the main concerns for any customer considering how to deal with their house banks is how to set up the connection in a standardized way, avoiding individual solutions and unnecessary complexities. To achieve this goal, streamlining the bank connectivity in a single channel is highly desirable. In this way, the complexity of building the connections is avoided. SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity works in a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) model, meaning that parts of the onboarding process are SAP-managed. The SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity team sets up the technical connections between the SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity infrastructure and the banks and maintains them.  The connection between the customer ERP system and SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is under the customer’s responsibility. Customers also have access to the SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity environment on SAP BTP and can monitor incoming and outgoing messages from their ERP system or SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity.

On the below architecture picture, you can see that no matter which channel is chosen for the bank connection, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is providing a single streamlined platform for the connections. The great news is that the maximum amount of bank connections is not limited and with a large amount of banks, the SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity membership fee cover it. Depending on the volume of transactions cost could increase.

 

Think of SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity as a reliable postman between your organization and your banks. It handles the exchange of various message formats, including ISO20022 standards and both end-of-day and intraday statements. Whether these formats originate from your side or the bank's side, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity ensures smooth delivery.

A popular use case involves integrating banks where you only need to download statements without initiating payments. This setup enables valuable processes like maintaining a full, real-time view of your cash position across all accounts.

One of SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity's standout features is its intuitive monitoring dashboard. The traffic light system provides instant visibility into your bank connections:

  • Green: Messages sent or received successfully
  • Yellow: Processed with warnings
  • Red: Failed transactions

The dashboard includes prefiltered views, allowing you to quickly focus on incoming messages, outgoing messages, or only those requiring attention. This streamlined approach makes daily monitoring and troubleshooting significantly easier for application users.

 

 SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity isn't just for bank connections. Many organizations use it to connect internal systems across subsidiaries, routing payment files and statements efficiently when direct connections aren't practical. It even supports non-SAP ERP systems and legacy SAP ECC environments through dedicated connectors, acting as a single gateway that reduces complexity and operational overhead.

 

 

Understanding Your Licensing Options

When implementing SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity, it's essential to understand the licensing structure to make informed decisions for your organization. The licensing model consists of two main components: membership fees and transaction-based charges.

 

Membership Packages

 Base Package

The Base package provides your SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity entitlement and allows connections to up to five banks, excluding any SWIFT integration. Connections through the member bank channel don't count toward this limit, giving you additional flexibility.

 Advanced Package

For organizations with more extensive banking needs, the Advanced package removes all restrictions on bank connections. It also includes entitlement for one Swift connection with a single BIC (Bank Identifier Code). While additional Swift costs don't apply through SAP, you'll still need to complete the standard Swift KYC (Know Your Customer) process. A significant advantage here is SAP's central billing arrangement with Swift – you pay SAP directly, and SAP handles the Swift payment.

 

Transaction Fees

 Transaction counting is straightforward but important to understand. When you send messages to banks, each line item within that message counts as one transaction. For example, processing a monthly HR salary payment with 10,000 employees means 10,000 transactions, calculated monthly.

 

Integration with other SAP modules

One question we hear frequently is: "How does SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity integrate with our existing SAP modules?" The good news? SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is designed to work seamlessly with your SAP ecosystem, connecting exactly where it adds the most value. SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity isn’t a standalone solution. It's built to enhance your existing SAP processes. Take treasury operations, for example. SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity supports the exchange of MT300 and MT320 messages for treasury deal confirmations with counterparties. This means your treasury transactions can be automatically created and settled, reducing manual effort and potential errors.

One of the most popular use cases we see is implementing a payment factory or in-house bank using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, advanced payment management or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, advanced payment management.

Here, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity acts as the communication backbone, handling all interactions between your banks and source systems. Think of it as the bridge that makes your centralized payment processing possible.

Furthermore, as you can see on the picture above, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is fully integrated with Cash Management  that enables comprehensive overview of the payment process with Monitor Payments dashboard and a release functionality with flexible release workflow setup options.

The SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity also enables the payment processes connection to advanced payment management and the In-House Bank. It is well integration into the Financial Accounting processing to reconcile open items via the Bank statement processing from an  end to end processes running in an integrated and automatized way.

To simplify, all modules that are generating payment messages or need to get status notifications, bank statements or other payment related messages are connected to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity via the Connector which is the central point of dealing with the distribution of messages or Bank related requests (like Instant Balance update) on the SAP ERP Systems.

 

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity operating model

One of the key factors when considering the bank connectivity is the ease and cost related to its operations. It has already been mentioned that SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity follows a SaaS model. The Onboarding Team takes care of the relevant settings between the customer SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity tenant and the banks. It is important to note that customers carry responsibility for their ERP settings related to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity and connection from ERP to SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity tenant. Both are well documented in the SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity documentation (Onboarding Process | SAP Help Portal) and do not require deep integration expertise but technical expertise. With SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity, customers can achieve efficiency in staffing costs and organizations’ overhead costs. No specific skills like integration experts or operation skills are required. Within the normal technical, functional, and business skills, the tool can be operated and used. After the implementation of SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity goes live, the support team can be reached any time 24/7 in a convenient way via an SAP for Me ticket. Outsourcing the bank connectivity setup and maintenance to SAP in this way enables the organizations to focus on their core business and improve their outcomes.

Scalability

With bank communications, the robustness of the solution and its scalability often come to mind. The implemented solutions must provide the option to scale with the growing success and volumes of the business. Luckily, SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity as a cloud solution is well suited to handle increasing traffic volumes. Nowadays, there are already implementations where SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity handles both small and huge number of line items within a single month.

Latest improvements

If you're a new SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is part of the Business Suite package S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is a continually evolving products. The SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity team is working continually to improve, enhance and deliver new features and functions which are identified or nominated by customers. One of the recent benefits is the ability of a customer to set up the connector between the S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition and SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity in a self-service way. This serves to decrease the required setup time due to less communication overhead.

Authors:
@PeterMeszaros & @lasse_becker
